name: Southern Cross Cable System
description: Transpacific cable connecting Australia and New Zealand to the United States via Hawaii

name: Australia-Japan Cable
description: Cable connecting Australia to Japan via Guam

name: PIPE Pacific Cable-1
description: Cable connecting Australia to Guam and Hawaii

name: Tasman Global Access
description: Cable connecting Australia and New Zealand

name: SEA-ME-WE 3
description: Southeast Asia - Middle East - Western Europe 3, connecting Asia to Europe

name: SEA-ME-WE 4
description: Southeast Asia - Middle East - Western Europe 4, connecting Asia to Europe

name: Asia America Gateway
description: Cable connecting Southeast Asia to the United States via Guam and Hawaii

name: Indigo Central
description: Cable connecting Australia to Singapore via Indonesia

name: Indigo West
description: Cable connecting Australia to India via Singapore

name: Asia Pacific Gateway
description: Cable connecting Japan, Korea, Taiwan, Hong Kong, and Southeast Asia

name: East Asia Crossing
description: Cable connecting Japan, Korea, and Southeast Asia

name: City to City
description: Pacnet ring cable system connecting major East Asian cities: Hong Kong, Manila, Taipei, Tokyo, Seoul with branch to Singapore

name: BiFrost
description: Meta/Amazon/Keppel/Telin Pacific cable — US West Coast to Singapore via Guam, Philippines and Indonesia. RFS Oct 2025. Main trunk 16,556 km.

name: Echo
description: Google/Meta transpacific cable — Singapore (Changi/Telstra) to US West Coast (Eureka CA) via Indonesia and Guam. Main trunk 16,206 km. RFS 2024–25.

name: Topaz
description: Google private cable — US Pacific Northwest (Vancouver/Port Alberni BC) to Japan (Takahagi/Ibaraki + Shima/Mie). ~10,000 km. RFS Jun 2024.

name: Tabua
description: Google (Starfish) South Pacific cable — Los Angeles to Sydney/Queensland via Hawaii and Fiji. Main trunk 13,323 km. RFS Q1 2026.
